questions.question: Kaggle provides free GPU compute of:
options: 30 hours/week (T4 or P100), resets Sunday UTC midnight
questions.explanation: 30 GPU hours/week is enough for most student training runs.

questions.question: T4 GPU on Kaggle has approximately:
To avoid wasting GPU quota:
options: Develop and debug on CPU first, switch to GPU only for training runs
questions.explanation: CPU development catches most bugs. GPU time is precious.

questions.question: Kaggle notebook outputs persist when:
options: Saved to /kaggle/working/ or explicitly to a dataset -- session /tmp is cleared on disconnect
questions.explanation: Only /kaggle/working/ survives. Save checkpoints and models there.

questions.question: Mixed precision (fp16) training:
options: Approximately doubles training speed and halves VRAM usage with minimal accuracy impact
questions.explanation: torch.autocast enables fp16. Hugely beneficial for large models.

questions.question: Internet must be enabled on Kaggle notebooks to:
options: Download packages (pip install), load HuggingFace models, call external APIs
questions.explanation: Settings -> Internet -> On. Competitions disable internet during submission.

questions.question: pin_memory=True in DataLoader:
options: Speeds up CPU-to-GPU data transfer by using page-locked host memory
questions.explanation: Small speedup for GPU training. Use with num_workers > 0.

questions.question: A Kaggle dataset is useful for:
options: Persisting data and model outputs across notebook sessions as read-only mounts
questions.explanation: Create a dataset from notebook output to reuse files without re-running.

questions.question: gradient_checkpointing=True saves GPU memory by:
options: Recomputing intermediate activations during backward pass instead of storing them
questions.explanation: Roughly halves VRAM usage at ~30% training speed cost. Enables training larger models.

questions.question: The Kaggle Accelerator setting for LLM fine-tuning should be:
options: GPU T4 x2 or P100 for VRAM; use QLoRA to fit within budget
questions.explanation: LLM fine-tuning needs maximum VRAM. T4 x2 gives 32GB.
